item.title: Teen attackers in mosque shooting were wallowing in nihilistic hate
item.description: "These subjects did not discriminate in who they hated," said Mark Remily, special agent in charge of the FBI's San Diego Field Office.

item.title: Several people watched San Diego attack live on video calls, recordings show
item.description: At least three people watched a livestream as gunmen filmed their deadly attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday. One viewer urged another to alert law enforcement, but it is not clear if anyone did.

item.title: Single Gen Z women outpacing men in home buying
item.description: Data from the National Association of Realtors shows that 35% of single women ages 18 to 26 are buying homes compared to just 18% of single Gen Z men. However, as a whole, the age group is struggling to keep up with others when it comes to owning a home. Jessica Lautz, the deputy chief economist at the National Association of Realtors, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Iran mulls latest U.S. proposal as Trump says war is in "final stages"
item.description: Iran says it's considering the latest U.S. peace offer, as President Trump says he's willing to wait "a couple of days" for a response.

item.title: What's holding the U.S. and Iran apart on a peace deal?
item.description: The Iranian government is reviewing the latest U.S. proposal to end the war. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean analyzes the situation.

item.title: A theater summer camp for adults
item.description: Summer Camp isn't just for kids anymore. Each year, more adults are heading back to camp for a chance to sing, dance and take a break from burnout. Gwen Baumgardner unpacks the growing trend for grown-ups.

item.title: Breaking down the Democrats' 2024 election autopsy report
item.description: The Democratic National Committee has released the long-awaited autopsy report on its 2024 election loss. CBS News political contributor Joel Payne breaks down the results.

item.title: Trump onboards more than 80 new immigration judges to speed up deportation cases
item.description: CBS News has learned that the Trump administration brought on more than 80 new federal immigration judges this week to speed up deportation cases. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the exclusive report.

item.title: CBS News Radio prepares for final sign off after nearly 100 years of broadcasting
item.description: CBS News Radio will sign off this Friday after nearly a century on the air. Bradley Blackburn sat down with familiar voices to look back and listen.

item.title: Is Trump's $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization fund" legal? Experts weigh in.
item.description: The new fund to provide payouts to those who say the legal system was "weaponized" against them raised immediate questions about its legality, implementation and enforcement.

item.title: House bill would ban use of federal money for DOJ's "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: GOP Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick and Democratic Rep. Tom Suozzi introduced a bill to ban the use of federal money for paying out claims under the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: U.S.-bound flight diverted over passenger from Congo amid Ebola restrictions
item.description: An Air France flight from Paris to Detroit was forced to divert to Montreal due to U.S. flight restrictions linked to the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: 5/21: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: New U.S. travel restrictions imposed over Ebola outbreak; fallout continues over DOJ's "anti-weaponization fund."

item.title: Sister of Cuban military conglomerate chief arrested in Florida, Rubio says
item.description: The sister of the head of a company that is accused of having close ties to Cuban government operations has been arrested in Miami, federal authorities announced.

item.title: SpaceX launch of more powerful Super Heavy-Starship rocket scrubbed at last minute
item.description: The new rocket features a host of upgrades intended to improve safety and performance of the world's most powerful rocket.

item.title: Organization aims to honor veterans after death with live "Taps" renditions
item.description: Americans might hear a bugle playing a piece called taps each Memorial Day. Tony Dokoupil has the story about an effort to guarantee that all veterans, when they die, are properly honored with it.

item.title: SpaceX scrubs test launch of upgraded Starship rocket
item.description: SpaceX has scrubbed a Thursday test launch of the latest version of its Starship rocket. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has the details.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dies at 41 after a severe illness, his family says
item.description: Kyle Busch's family earlier Thursday announced he had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: New weight-loss drug Retatrutide showing stronger results than current options
item.description: A new weight-loss drug, so new it is not on the market yet, is showing promising results in trials, doing much more than what current medications can. Dr. Jon LaPook breaks it down.

item.title: CBS News Radio, an American institution, will sign off for the last time
item.description: Over the decades, radio's listeners drifted to television, podcasts and streaming. On Friday, CBS News Radio will sign off for the last time. Mark Strassmann takes a look back at the institution.

item.title: Todd Blanche tries to sell Republicans on "anti-weaponization fund"
item.description: The Justice Department has a new $1.776 billion fund, designated to pay people who allege that politics played a role in their prosecutions under the Biden administration. It's a settlement without precedent, drawn up by the president's own appointees after he sued the government. Weijia Jiang has more details.

item.title: Stormy week for travelers heading into Memorial Day weekend
item.description: A record-breaking 45-million Americans are expected to be on the move this holiday weekend, and it could get messy, especially in the East, across the South, and into Texas. Rob Marciano is tracking the forecast.

item.title: Trump lawyer-turned-critic Michael Cohen to apply for "anti-weaponization fund"
item.description: ​Michael Cohen, a Trump lawyer-turned-critic, is planning to apply for money from the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization fund," he told CBS News.

item.title: Flight diverts to Canada over Ebola concerns: "Suddenly all the flight attendants had face masks on"
item.description: A flight headed for Detroit made a last-minute diversion to Canada on Wednesday night over Ebola concerns stemming from a passenger who wasn't supposed to be on board. CBS News' Gwen Baumgardner and Nicole Sganga have more.

item.title: Republican lawmakers seek clarity on Trump's "anti-weaponization fund"
item.description: A growing number of lawmakers are speaking out against the Justice Department's $1.7 billion "anti-weaponization fund." CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Obama wins presidency in 2008
item.description: Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage of the election of the first Black president of the U.S., Barack Obama.

item.title: Former GOP Sen. Lamar Alexander says DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund "smells pretty fishy to me"
item.description: Tennessee Republican Lamar Alexander served as a U.S. senator, governor, and secretary of education, and ran for president in 1996 and 2000. He chronicles his six decades in politics in his new book, "The Education of a Senator: From JFK to Trump." Alexander joins to assess the state of the GOP under President Trump.

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene says Trump is targeting Republicans who voted to release Epstein files
item.description: After Rep. Thomas Massie's defeat in his Kentucky primary, another Republican politician whom President Trump targeted spoke out in his defense: former Georgia Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene. Greene joins "The Takeout" to unpack the effort to oust Massie and the GOP's future.

item.title: 5/21: CBS Evening News
item.description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies at 41; an Ebola scare causes travel chaos.

item.title: DOJ onboards largest-ever class of immigration judges in deportation push
item.description: The additions come after the ouster of dozens of immigration judges across the country by the Trump administration over the past year.
